Active Work Architecture
─[ Field Fix Submit Skill ]─ ┐

Field Fix Submit Skill

New skill /field-fix-submit that packages a local field-repair fix with structured metadata and submits it as a PR against the source framework repo. Deployed to all instances via harness sync.

Goal

Any Lore instance operator can contribute a verified fix back to the source repo with a single skill invocation, without leaving the instance or manually constructing PR metadata.

Flow

  1. Operator confirms fix is ready for submission
  2. Skill runs validate-consistency.sh — bail if it fails
  3. Skill identifies modified harness-owned files (diff against last sync baseline)
  4. Skill strips instance-specific data (security convention enforcement)
  5. Skill generates structured PR body from the metadata schema (Phase 1)
  6. Skill prompts operator for bug description, root cause, and test evidence
  7. Creates GitHub issue via gh issue create (or links existing issue if provided)
  8. Creates branch + PR via gh against source repo (lorehq/lore)
  9. Operator reviews the PR body and approves submission

Key Design Decisions

  • Operator approval at two points — before packaging (step 1) and before submission (step 9). No silent submissions.
  • Fork-based workflow — Skill forks the source repo to the operator’s GitHub account (if not already forked), pushes to the fork, and creates a cross-fork PR. Standard OSS contribution model.
  • gh CLI dependency — Requires gh authenticated with repo scope. Skill checks for gh availability and auth status before proceeding.
  • Version-keyed diffs — Diff is tagged with the Lore version the instance is running, so the triage pipeline knows which base to apply it against.

Scope

Target repo: /home/andrew/Github/lore (source framework)

Files to create:

  • .lore/skills/field-fix-submit/SKILL.md — the skill definition (30-80 lines per convention)

Constraints:

  • Skill must work from Claude Code, Cursor, and OpenCode instances
  • No lore- prefix — this is an operator skill, not a harness command (sync would overwrite it). Use field-fix-submit naming.
  • Must enforce security convention: scan diff for hardcoded paths, env vars, secrets, operator-specific data before submission

Security Checks

The skill performs these checks before submission:

  • No absolute paths from the instance filesystem in the diff
  • No environment variable values (only references)
  • No operator identity information (usernames, emails, account IDs)
  • No files outside harness-owned directories (hooks/, scripts/, lib/, .lore/skills/, .lore/agents/)
  • validate-consistency.sh passes

Deliverables

  • Skill file: .lore/skills/field-fix-submit/SKILL.md
  • Skill registered in .lore/registry/skills.json
  • Skill works end-to-end: instance fix -> GitHub PR against source
  • Documentation: brief usage section in the skill file itself

Dependencies

  • Phase 1 (metadata format) must be complete — skill generates metadata per that schema

Success Criteria

  • Skill invocation from a test instance creates a well-formed PR against source
  • PR body contains valid YAML metadata block parseable by the triage workflow
  • Security checks catch and reject submissions containing instance-specific data
  • Operator can review and approve/cancel at both checkpoints
  • gh auth failure produces a clear error message, not a stack trace
  • Skill file is under 80 lines

Test Plan

# In lore source repo after adding skill
bash scripts/validate-consistency.sh

# Manual: invoke skill in a test instance, verify PR appears on GitHub
PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
